1: Using its tough and impressive horn, the user rams into the target with no letup.
2: The user calls out its underlings to pummel the target. Critical hits land more easily.
3: The user vibrates its wings to generate a damaging sound wave. It may also lower the target's Sp. Def stat.
4: The user slashes at the foe by crossing its scythes or claws as if they were a pair of scissors.
5: The user attacks with a sinister beam of light. It may also confuse the target.
6: After making its attack, the user rushes back to switch places with a party Pokémon in waiting.
7: The user crushes its foes by rolling over them. This attack may make the target flinch.
8: The user bites the target. If the target is holding a Berry, the user eats it and gains its effect.
9: The foe is attacked with powdery scales blown by wind. It may also raise all the user's stats.
10: While resisting, the user attacks the opposing Pokémon. The targets' Sp. Atk stat is reduced.
11: The foe is stabbed twice by a pair of stingers. It may also poison the target.
12: The target is slashed with scythes or claws. Its power increases if it hits in succession.
13: The user drains the target's blood. The user's HP is restored by half the damage taken by the target.
14: Sharp spikes are shot at the target in rapid succession. They hit two to five times in a row.
15: The user calls out its underlings to shield its body, raising its Defense and Sp. Def stats.
16: The user calls out its underlings to heal it. The user regains up to half of its max HP.
17: The user performs a beautiful dance. It boosts the user's Sp. Atk, Sp. Def, and Speed stats.
18: The user scatters irritating powder to draw attention to itself. Opponents aim only at the user.
19: The user ensnares the target with thin, gooey silk so it can't flee from battle.
20: The foe is bound with silk blown from the user's mouth. This silk reduces the target's Speed.
21: The user stares at flashing lights to focus its mind, drastically raising its Sp. Atk stat.
22: The user turns the foe's power against it. It does more damage the higher the target's Attack stat.
23: The user lets loose a pitch-black shock wave at its target. It may also lower the target's accuracy.
24: The user crunches up the target with sharp fangs. It may also lower the target's Defense stat.
25: The user releases a horrible aura imbued with dark thoughts. It may also make the target flinch.
26: This move enables the user to attack first. It fails if the target is not readying an attack, however.
27: The user slashes the target the instant an opportunity arises. Critical hits land more easily.
28: The target is bitten with viciously sharp fangs. It may make the target flinch.
29: The user draws up to the foe disarmingly, then throws a sucker punch. It hits without fail.
30: The user yells as if it is ranting about something, making the target's Sp. Atk stat decrease.
31: If the target has already taken some damage in the same turn, this attack's power is doubled.
32: If the user moves after the target, this attack's power will be doubled.
33: An attack move that inflicts double damage if used on a target that is switching out of battle.
34: The user attacks and steals the foe's held item simultaneously. It can't steal if the user holds an item.
35: The user slaps down the target's held item, preventing that item from being used in the battle.
36: The user gets all the party Pokémon to attack the foe. The more party Pokémon, the more damage.
37: The user flings its held item at the target to attack. Its power and effects depend on the item.
38: This attack's power increases the more the target has powered up with stat changes.
39: Opposing Pokémon are dragged into a world of total darkness that makes them sleep.
40: It prevents the target from using its held item. Its Trainer is also prevented from using items on it.
41: The user feigns crying to fluster the target, harshly lowering its Sp. Def stat.
42: Flattery is used to confuse the target. However, it also raises the target's Sp. Atk stat.
43: The user sharpens its claws to boost its Attack stat and accuracy.
44: The user faints when using this move. In return, it harshly lowers the target's Attack and Sp. Atk.
45: The user stimulates its brain by thinking bad thoughts. It sharply raises the user's Sp. Atk.
46: The user suppresses the target and makes its move go last.
47: The user steals the effects of any healing or stat-changing move the foe attempts to use.
48: The user trades held items with the target faster than the eye can follow.
49: The target is taunted into a rage that allows it to use only attack moves for three turns.
50: The user torments and enrages the foe, making it incapable of using the same move twice in a row.
51: The user blasts the target with power that distorts even time. The user must rest on the next turn.
52: Comets are summoned down from the sky. The attack's recoil harshly reduces the user's Sp. Atk stat.
53: The user rampages and attacks for two to three turns. It then becomes confused, however.
54: The user tackles the foe while exhibiting overwhelming menace. It may also make the target flinch.
55: The user tears the target along with the space around it. Critical hits land more easily.
56: The target is attacked with a shock wave generated by the user's gaping mouth.
57: The user slashes the target with huge, sharp claws.
58: The user knocks away the target and drags out another Pokémon in its party. In the wild, the battle ends.
59: The user exhales a mighty gust that inflicts damage. It may also leave the target with paralysis.
60: The user attacks its target by hitting it with brutal strikes. The target is hit twice in a row.
61: The user whips up a vicious tornado to tear at the opposing team. It may also make targets flinch.
62: This attack hits the target with a shock wave of pure rage. This attack always inflicts 40 HP damage.
63: The user vigorously performs a mystic, powerful dance that boosts its Attack and Speed stats.
64: The user charges at its foe, surrounding itself with lightning. It may also leave the target paralyzed.
65: A wicked thunderbolt is dropped on the foe to inflict damage. It may also leave the target paralyzed.
66: The user electrifies itself, then charges at the foe. It causes considerable damage to the user as well.
67: The user fires an electric blast like a cannon to inflict damage and cause paralysis.
68: The user throws down a giant thunderbolt. It does more damage if influenced by an enormous flame.
69: A strong electric blast is loosed at the target. It may also leave the target with paralysis.
70: The user shrouds itself in electricity and smashes into its foe. It also damages the user a little.
71: A flare of electricity is loosed to strike the area around the user. It may also cause paralysis.
72: The target is punched with an electrified fist. It may also leave the target with paralysis.
73: After making its attack, the user rushes back to switch places with a party Pokémon in waiting.
74: The user throws an electrically charged tackle at the target. It may also leave the target with paralysis.
75: The user bites with electrified fangs. It may also make the target flinch or leave it with paralysis.
76: The user strikes the target with a quick jolt of electricity. This attack cannot be evaded.
77: The user captures and attacks foes by using an electric net, which lowers their Speed stat.
78: The user fires a concentrated bundle of electricity. It may also raise the user's Sp. Atk stat.
79: A jolt of electricity is hurled at the foe to inflict damage. It may also leave the target with paralysis.
80: The user hurls an electric orb at the foe. It does more damage the faster the user is.
81: The user boosts the power of the Electric move it uses next. It also raises the user's Sp. Def stat.
82: The user levitates using electrically generated magnetism for five turns.
83: A weak electric charge is launched at the target. It causes paralysis if it hits.
84: The user focuses its mind before launching a punch. It will fail if the user is hit before it is used.
85: The target is attacked with a knee kick from a jump. If it misses, the user is hurt instead.
86: The user fights the foe up close without guarding itself. It also cuts the user's Defense and Sp. Def.
87: The user heightens its mental focus and unleashes its power. It may also lower the target's Sp. Def.
88: The user attacks the target with great power. However, it also lowers the user's Attack and Defense.
89: The user delivers a double chop with its forearms crossed. Critical hits land more easily.
90: The user punches the target with full, concentrated power. It confuses the target if it hits.
91: The user swings and hits with its strong and heavy fist. It lowers the user's Speed, however.
92: The user jumps up high, then strikes with a kick. If the kick misses, the user hurts itself.
93: The user looses a blast of aura power from deep within its body. This move is certain to hit.
94: The user attacks by slicing with its long horns. The target's stat changes don't affect the damage.
95: The user cuts with its long horn. The odd power contained in it does physical damage to the foe.
96: The user attacks the target with an uppercut thrown skyward with force.
97: The user grabs the target and recklessly dives for the ground. It also hurts the user slightly.
98: The user attacks with a swift chop. It can also break any barrier such as Light Screen and Reflect.
99: An energy-draining punch. The user's HP is restored by half the damage taken by the target.
100: The user attacks last. In return, this throw move is guaranteed not to miss.
101: The user throws the target and drags out another Pokémon in its party. In the wild, the battle ends.
102: The target is attacked with a shock wave. It may also leave the target with paralysis.
103: The user attacks the target's legs swiftly, reducing the target's Speed stat.
104: An attack move that inflicts double the damage if the user has been hurt by the foe in the same turn.
105: The user lashes out with a quick, spinning kick. It may also make the target flinch.
106: This attack inflicts big damage on a sleeping target. It also wakes the target up, however.
107: The target is attacked with a sharp chop. Critical hits land more easily.
108: The user throws a punch at blinding speed. It is certain to strike first.
109: The user attacks with a punch that can shatter a rock. It may also lower the foe's Defense stat.
110: The user strikes the target with a fierce blow. This attack always results in a critical hit.
111: The user whirls its fists to send a wave of pure vacuum at the target. This move always goes first.
112: The target is quickly kicked twice in succession using both feet.
113: The user looses a flurry of open-palmed arm thrusts that hit two to five times in a row.
114: A consecutive three-kick attack that becomes more powerful with each successive hit.
115: A retaliation move that counters any physical attack, inflicting double the damage taken.
116: The user risks all to attack the foe. The user faints but does damage equal to its HP.
117: A powerful low kick that makes the foe fall over. It inflicts greater damage on heavier foes.
118: An all-out attack that becomes more powerful the less HP the user has.
119: The target is thrown using the power of gravity. It inflicts damage equal to the user's level.
120: The user tenses its muscles to bulk up its body, boosting both its Attack and Defense stats.
121: It enables the user to evade all attacks. Its chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.
122: The user protects itself and its allies from priority moves. If may fail if used in succession.
123: With a fiery forehead, the user hurls itself at the foe. It lowers the user's Defense, Sp. Def, and Speed.
124: The target is razed by a fiery explosion. The user must rest on the next turn, however.
125: The user attacks in an explosive fury. The lower the user's HP, the less powerful this attack becomes.
126: The user attacks the target at full power. The attack's recoil sharply reduces the user's Sp. Atk stat.
127: The user attacks by engulfing the foe in a beautiful, yet intense, blue flame. It may also burn the foe.
128: The foe is attacked with an intense blast of all-consuming fire. It may also leave the target with a burn.

130: The target becomes trapped within a maelstrom of fire that rages for four to five turns.
131: The user brings down a giant flame. It does more damage if influenced by an enormous thunderbolt.
132: The user attacks by exhaling hot breath on the opposing team. It may also leave targets with a burn.

137: The user launches a kick with a high critical-hit ratio. It may also leave the target with a burn.

139: An inferno of scarlet flames torches everything around the user. It may leave targets with a burn.
140: The target is punched with a fiery fist. It may leave the target with a burn.
141: The user attacks the foe with a bursting flame. It also damages Pokémon next to the target.
142: The user bites with flame-cloaked fangs. It may also make the target flinch or leave it burned.
143: The user cloaks itself in fire and charges at the target. It may also leave the target with a burn.

146: The target is attacked with small flames. It may also leave the target with a burn.
147: The target becomes trapped within a fierce vortex of fire that rages for four to five turns.

151: The user shoots a sinister, bluish-white flame at the target to inflict a burn.

153: The user tucks in its wings and charges from a low altitude. The user also takes serious damage.

155: A vortex of air is shot at the target to inflict damage. Critical hits land more easily.
156: The user soars, then strikes on the second turn. It can also be used for flying to any familiar town.

159: The user attacks with a blade of air that slices even the sky. It may also make the target flinch.

168: The target is jabbed with a sharply pointed beak or horn.

180: The user extends its shadow and attacks the target from behind. This move always goes first.

184: The target is exposed to a sinister ray that triggers confusion.
185: A move that works differently for the Ghost type than for all the other types.
186: When this move is used, if the user faints, the foe that landed the knockout hit also faints.
187: If the user faints, the user's grudge fully depletes the PP of the foe's move that knocked it out.
188: A sleeping target sees a nightmare that inflicts some damage every turn.
